1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 081 — **two substantive prover lanes** building on the iter-080 structural unblocks; Lane 3 deferred pending Mathlib upstream).
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the path: Phase A (cohomology) + Phase B (differentials) + Phase C (Picard scheme, multi-step gap-fill — C0 substantially complete with `instIsMonoidal_W` deferred, C1 scheduled iter-082+, C2 iter-083+, C3 iter-086+) + Phase D (genus / Jacobian / instances, closed-by-reduction since iter-073) + Phase E (Abel–Jacobi, closed iter-073).
18	
19	Iter-080 ran three lanes; all three made structural progress without regression but none hit the closure target. Net 14 → 14. Highlights:
20	- **Lane 1 BasicOpenCech**: named-per-i `letI` refactor landed (L920–949). The iter-076/078/079 confirmed blocker `Pi.smul_apply` not firing against `RingHom.toModule`-built anonymous per-i instances is **closed**. S2–S8 mechanical chain (~50 LOC inline) pending.
21	- **Lane 2 Differentials**: Route A advance — `SheafOfModules.hom_ext` + explicit `show` replaces stale iter-076 simp step; after `ext U b`, blocked at inline-`d_target` matcher pathology. Three unblock routes documented inline: (a) `set d_target`, (b) refactor-subagent, (c) Mathlib-shape `Derivation.postcomp_comp` helper.
22	- **Lane 3 Modules/Monoidal**: confirmed Mathlib gap — `(M ⊗_psh N).stalk x ≅ M.stalk x ⊗_{R₀.stalk x} N.stalk x` absent for varying-ring `PresheafOfModules R₀`. Deferred pending upstream PR.
23	
24	Current project state (independently verified this plan pass via `sorry_analyzer.py` + `lean_diagnostic_messages`):
25	
26	| File | Sorries | Compiles? | Phase | Iter-081 plan |
27	|---|---|---|---|---|
28	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | 6 | yes | A | **Lane 1: complete S2–S8 mechanical chain on top of iter-080's `letI` refactor to close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1176)** |
29	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | 5 | yes | B | **Lane 2: introduce Mathlib-shape helper `Derivation'.postcomp_comp` + close `cotangentExactSeq_structure` via Route (c) + second gap-fill `exact_iff_stalkwise` (1-for-1 sorry shift)** |
30	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` | 1 | yes | C0 | off-limits (deferred pending Mathlib upstream — `stalk_tensorObj` for varying-ring R₀) |
31	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | 1 | yes | D done | off-limits (`nonempty_jacobianWitness` deferred to iter-086+) |
32	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | yes | C-rep | off-limits |
33	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 0 | yes | E done | off-limits |
34	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` | 0 | yes | D done | off-limits |
35	| **Total syntactic** | **14** | — | | |
36	
37	## Current Objectives
38	
39	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
40	
41	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — complete S2–S8 to close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`
42	
43	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L502, L826, L854 (3 dead-end substeps — stay), **L1176 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** (this lane's target), L1221 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1250 `h_loc_exact`.
44	
45	**Goal.** Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1176): the f-side per-summand R-linearity decomposition through the 5-layer functor stack. Iter-080 landed the named-per-i `letI` refactor at L920–949 — `Pi.smul_apply` now fires directly on the post-`funext j` goal. The remaining work is the mechanical S2–S8 chain (~50 LOC inline).
46	
47	**State to build on (DO NOT modify).** These iter-078/079/080 commits are essential prerequisites:
48	- `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L418 above the docstring of `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf` (iter-078). Required because *any* tactic step inside the `have h_diff_pi_smul_f` block triggers `HSMul R (Z_i j) ?_` synthesis whose whnf cost exceeds 200000 at the theorem head.
49	- `intro r y; funext j` at L1093–1094 (iter-078). Cleanly reduces the goal to the componentwise form.
50	- **The named-per-i `letI` block at L920–949 (iter-080)**: `letI perI_n : ∀ i, Module R (Z_n i) := fun i => by …; letI h_mod_pi_n : Module R (∀ i, Z_n i) := Pi.module _ _ _` for n = 1, 2, 3. Both `letI` — this is critical (with `have h_mod_pi_n`, the body is opaque and downstream `h_mod_X_n` constructions break). Byte-for-byte equivalent to the iter-072 anonymous construction; only typeclass-visibility changes.
51	- The iter-079 `change h_mod_pi₂.toSMul.smul …` step at L1115–1118 is now **unnecessary** — `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]` directly produces the desired form. The prover may delete this step (or keep it as a no-op for documentation; prover's discretion).
52	
53	**Mathematical content.** The statement `h_diff_pi_smul_f` packages: for `r : R` and `y : ∀ i, Z₁ i`, the f-side cochain differential `scK₀.f` applied to the smul `r • y` equals `r • (scK₀.f y)`, computed via the canonical `e_i : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i) ≃ scK₀.X_i` linear equivalences. The R-action on `∀ i, Z_n i` is defined componentwise via `Pi.module`, where each `Z_n i = Γ(C.left, V_i)` is an R-algebra via the restriction map.
54	
55	**Recipe.**
56	
57	After `intro r y; funext j; simp only [Pi.smul_apply]` (the iter-080 `change` step may be deleted), the goal is:
58	```
59	e₂ ((scK₀.f).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j = r • e₂ ((scK₀.f).hom (e₁.symm y)) j
60	```
61	
62	**Phase A (target ~20 LOC — expose the alternating-sum form):**
63	1. **S2 (5-layer dsimp; verified working iter-080):** `dsimp only [scK₀, K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor, toModuleKSheaf, toModuleKPresheaf_obj]` exposes `(scK₀.f).hom = objD X (prev n)` where `X := (evalOp.obj P) ∘ (mk _ cech).rightOp`, `P := (sheafToPresheaf _).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`.
64	2. **S3 (objD identification; the hardest pain point):** Use `AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD` to identify the differential. The `K₀.d (prev n) n` opacity (from `ComplexShape.up ℕ`) requires `CochainComplex.of_d_eq_succ` after `(up ℕ).Rel (prev n) n` case-split. Suggestion: introduce intermediate `have hd : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).Rel (prev n) n := by rfl` *before* invoking `of_d_eq_succ`. Explicit-argument instantiation may be required.
65	3. **S4 (alternating-sum decomposition):** Result: `objD X m = ∑ k : Fin (m+2), (-1)^k • X.δ k` where `X.δ k = X.map (op (SimplexCategory.δ k))`.
66	
67	**Phase B (target ~30 LOC — per-summand R-linearity):**
68	4. **S5 (`Pi.smul_apply` on LHS):** With the named-per-i refactor in place, this fires: evaluates `(r • y) (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom) = perI₁ (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom) .smul r (y (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom))` (i.e., `r • y (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom)`).
69	5. **S6 (`Finset.smul_sum`):** `rw [Finset.smul_sum]` distributes `r •` across the alternating sum on RHS.
70	6. **S7 (per-summand at fixed k):** `apply Finset.sum_congr rfl; intro k _`. For each `k`, the j-component of `X.δ k` is `Pi.π Z₁ (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom) ≫ (P.map (φ_k j).op)` via `CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct.evalOp_obj_map` + `Pi.lift`. Per-component R-linearity follows from `RingHom.map_mul` (R-algebra factor distributivity) + `← C.left.presheaf.map_comp` collapsing the two restrictions `(V_{j∘δ_k} ≤ U).op ≫ (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_k}).op` into `(V_j ≤ U).op`.
71	7. **S8:** Close by `rfl` or `ring`.
72	
73	**Partial-credit fallback.** If Phase A succeeds but Phase B stalls: leave the per-summand `Finset.sum_congr` block as a `?_` placeholder and document the per-summand goal in the body. This does NOT reduce the file's sorry count (the `?_` IS the sorry) but gives iter-082 a clean handoff. Do not stop early without writing the documentation.
74	
75	**Approaches ruled out iter-076/078/079/080** (do NOT re-attempt):
76	- `rw [Pi.smul_apply]` against the anonymous-builder `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul` *without* the iter-080 `letI` refactor — "did not find the pattern". With the refactor, it fires.
77	- `letI` parallel instance to bypass `h_mod_pi₂` anonymity without making `h_mod_pi_n` itself a `letI` — confirmed iter-079.
78	- `exact map_smul (scK₀.f.hom) r _` — `scK₀.f` is k-linear, not R-linear; the R-linearity is the *construct*.
79	- `module` tactic — not a pure linear-combination shape; sides involve presheaf restriction maps.
80	- `rfl` after S2's `dsimp` — sides are not defeq without `(up ℕ).Rel` case-split + `Pi.lift` decomposition.
81	- `show e₂ (…) j = r • (e₂ (…)) j` — HSMul synthesis on `Z₂ j` alone (no standalone R-action; only on the product via `h_mod_pi₂`).
82	- `exact (LinearEquiv.map_smul e₂ r _)` — `e₂` is `k`-linear, not `R`-linear.
83	
84	**Hard constraints**:
85	- **Preserve the iter-080 `letI` refactor at L920–949 byte-for-byte** (both `letI perI_n` AND `letI h_mod_pi_n := Pi.module _ _ _` for n = 1, 2, 3). Do not revert to `have h_mod_pi_n` — this would re-introduce the iter-076/078/079 blocker.
86	- **Preserve `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L418** (iter-078).
87	- **Preserve `funext j` at L1094** (iter-078).
88	- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (user policy 2026-05-11). The S2–S8 chain must run inline inside `h_diff_pi_smul_f`'s body. No extraction into thin helpers across iterations.
89	- **No new axioms.**
90	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation** of candidate bodies. `lean_diagnostic_messages` after each save IS allowed (standard dev feedback loop). `lean_multi_attempt` for position-bound tactic-attempt previews is also allowed.
91	- Sorry-count budget: starts at 6; target **≤ 5** (close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`); hard cap **6** (no regression).
92	- The L502 / L826 / L854 sorries are dead-ends — out of scope.
93	- `g_R.map_smul'` (L1221) and `h_loc_exact` (L1250) are downstream — out of scope (gated on this lane).
94	
95	**Mathlib references confirmed iter-080** (verify via `lean_local_search`):
96	- `CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct.evalOp_obj_map`
97	- `AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD`
98	- `CochainComplex.of_d_eq_succ`, `CochainComplex.next`
99	- `Pi.smul_apply`, `Finset.smul_sum`, `Finset.sum_congr`, `RingHom.map_mul`, `presheaf.map_comp`.
100	- Project-local `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality` (`StructureSheafModuleK.lean` L161; the R-level analogue is what S7 needs).
101	
102	**Blueprint**: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` § "Čech acyclicity for the structure sheaf on affine basic-open covers". The per-summand R-linearity is internal to `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf` and not a top-level declaration with its own blueprint label.
103	
104	### 2. `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` — close `cotangentExactSeq_structure` via Route (c)
105	
106	**File status.** 5 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L122 `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf`, **L522 `cotangentExactSeq_structure`** (this lane's target), L806 `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega`, L823 `cotangent_at_section`, L965 `serre_duality_genus`. The iter-079 gap-fill `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf` is fully closed at L437–443 (preserve byte-for-byte).
107	
108	**Goal.** Close the single absorbed sorry inside `cotangentExactSeq_structure` at L522 (the conjunction `h_zero ∧ h_exact ∧ h_epi`). Use **Route (c)** — introduce Mathlib-shape helper `Derivation'.postcomp_comp` to sidestep the inline-`d_target` matcher pathology that blocked iter-080's Route A advance. The chain collapses to `d_target.postcomp ((pushforward _).map β.val) = 0` via `Universal.fac`, avoiding `ext U b` entirely.
109	
110	**Why this is now reachable.** Three iter-079/080 deliverables make this tractable:

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
94	
95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
130	
131	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
132	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
133	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
134	   - Concrete next step for the next session
135	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
136	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)

37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1
98	
99	/-- Iter-055: producer for `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` from existence of basic-open
100	Čech-acyclic cover.

400	
401	This is the structural skeleton common to *every* `IsCechAcyclicCover` instance
402	proof: the actual mathematical content lives in establishing
403	`(cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n` for each `n > 0`. -/
404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
406	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
407	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
408	    {n : ℕ} (h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n) :
409	    Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) :=
410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology
411	
412	-- Iter-078: lifted to 800000 because the iter-078 prover work on
413	-- `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (per-summand R-linearity of the Čech differential)
414	-- requires further tactic steps inside the deeply-nested `have` block;
415	-- each additional rewrite touching the `(r • _)` smul triggers
416	-- `HSMul R (Z_i j) ?_` synthesis whose `whnf` cost exceeds the default
417	-- 200000 budget at the theorem head.
418	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
419	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
420	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
421	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
422	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
423	schemes in our setting.
424	
425	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
426	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
427	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
428	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
429	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
430	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
431	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
432	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
433	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
434	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
435	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
436	
437	The structural skeleton is:
438	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
439	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
440	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
441	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
442	
443	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
444	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
445	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
446	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
447	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
448	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
449	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
450	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
451	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
452	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
453	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
454	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
455	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
456	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
457	
458	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
459	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/
460	theorem basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf
461	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
462	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
463	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
464	    IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)
465	        (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) where
466	  subsingleton_cechCohomology n hn := by
467	    -- Step 0 (structural): reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to
468	    -- `ExactAt n` of the underlying Čech cochain complex via the iter-060
469	    -- helper `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`.
470	    refine cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt (n := n) ?_
471	    -- The goal is now
472	    --   `(cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)).ExactAt n`.
473	    -- Iter-061 decomposition: the Stacks 01ED extra-degeneracy + local-to-global
474	    -- argument has three substeps tracked by separate labelled `sorry`s.
475	    --
476	    -- ===========================================================================
477	    -- Substep (a) — Extra-degeneracy on the slice cover of `D(f)`.
478	    -- ===========================================================================
479	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover of `D(f)` by the family
480	    --   `{basicOpenCover s f' ⊓ D(f)}_{f' ∈ s}`
481	    -- contains `D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)` itself (at index `f' = f`), so the
482	    -- terminal vertex maps to `D(f)` via the identity section
483	    -- `D(f) → D(f)`. By `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
484	    -- (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L92),
485	    -- the augmented Čech simplicial object on this slice has an
486	    -- `ExtraDegeneracy`. By `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
487	    -- (`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L328), the associated
488	    -- alternating-face-map chain complex is homotopy-equivalent to the
489	    -- constant complex on the augmentation. Op-passage through
490	    -- `CochainComplex.opEquivalence` then yields cochain-level exactness in
491	    -- positive degrees. We state substep (a) as a concrete `ExactAt n`
492	    -- claim on the slice cover Čech cochain complex:
493	    have h_a : ∀ (f : s),
494	        (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
495	            (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
496	              C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))).ExactAt n := by
497	      intro f
498	      -- The terminal vertex of the slice cover is at `f' = f`:
499	      --   `basicOpenCover s f ⊓ C.left.basicOpen f.1 = D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)`.
500	      -- Plug into `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` with `i₀ := f`,
501	      -- extract `homotopyEquiv`, op-pass, read off `ExactAt n` for `0 < n`.
502	      sorry  -- substep (a) infrastructure: augmented Čech simplicial object
503	             -- + extraDegeneracyCech + homotopyEquiv + CochainComplex.opEquivalence
504	    -- ===========================================================================
505	    -- Substep (b) — Localised-Čech identification.
506	    -- ===========================================================================
507	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover Čech cochain complex above is
508	    -- isomorphic (in `ModuleCat k`) to the localisation at `f` of the
509	    -- original Čech cochain complex `K`. Cochain-factor-wise:
510	    --   `(slice cover).X^n = ∏_x F(⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k) ⊓ D(f))`
511	    --                      = `∏_x F(D(f · ∏ k, (x k).1))`              (Stacks identity)
512	    --                      = `∏_x Localization.Away (f · ∏ k, (x k).1) Γ(C.left, U)`
513	    --                      = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k))[1/f]`
514	    --                      = `K.X^n [1/f]`.
515	    -- The first equality is iter-057 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod`)
516	    -- applied to the augmented index `{f} ∪ image(x)`. The second is
517	    -- iter-059 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization`). The third is
518	    -- the universal property of localisation (transitivity of localisation:
519	    -- `R[1/(f·g)] = R[1/g][1/f]` when `f` is also among the generators).
520	    -- The differential identification uses `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`
521	    -- (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean` L56) to transport
522	    -- the cochain-level identification to the slice differential.
523	    --
524	    -- ===========================================================================
525	    -- Substep (c) — Local-to-global via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
526	    -- ===========================================================================
527	    -- The combined data from substeps (a) and (b) — per-`f` exactness at
528	    -- degree `n` of the slice cover Čech complex, which by substep (b) is
529	    -- the localisation of `K.sc n` — fits the hypothesis of
530	    -- `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ...`
531	    -- (`Mathlib/RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean` L173) viewing
532	    -- the cochain factors as `Γ(C.left, U)`-modules through their natural
533	    -- module structure. The conclusion is `Function.Exact ⇑(K.sc n).f ⇑(K.sc n).g`,
534	    -- which converts back to `K.ExactAt n` via
535	    -- `ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact` and
536	    -- `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff`.
537	    --
538	    -- This combined assembly remains as a single transient sorry: it
539	    -- requires constructing the cochain-level localisation iso (substep (b)
540	    -- ≈ a `noncomputable def` for the localised Čech short complex plus
541	    -- its identification iso) and routing the ring-base mismatch
542	    -- (`(K.sc n).f.hom` is `k`-linear; `exact_of_isLocalized_span` consumes
543	    -- `Γ(C.left, U)`-linear maps — they coincide as set-functions, but the
544	    -- type-level reconciliation is non-trivial).
545	    --
546	    -- For iter-061, substep (a)'s claim is concretely stated above and
547	    -- substep (b)+(c)'s assembly remains as the second labelled sorry below.
548	    -- We perform the mechanical conversion `K.ExactAt n ↔ Function.Exact ⇑f ⇑g`
549	    -- before the sorry, so the remaining content is precisely the
550	    -- "transport per-f exactness through the localisation iso then apply
551	    -- the local-to-global principle" assembly step.
552	    rw [HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff,
553	        ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact]
554	    -- Goal:
555	    --   `Function.Exact ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((K).sc n).f)
556	    --                   ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((K).sc n).g)`
557	    -- where `K := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)`.
558	    -- ===========================================================================
559	    -- Iter-062 substantive scaffolding (a): convert `h_a` into `Function.Exact` form.
560	    -- ===========================================================================
561	    -- The same `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff` +
562	    -- `ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact` chain that
563	    -- converts our goal also converts each per-`f` slice-cover `ExactAt n`
564	    -- hypothesis into a per-`f` `Function.Exact` statement at the
565	    -- function-level. This is closed under `← rw` (no new sorry).
566	    have h_a_fun : ∀ (f : s),
567	        Function.Exact
568	          ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc
569	              (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
570	                (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
571	                  C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))) n).f)
572	          ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc
573	              (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
574	                (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
575	                  C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))) n).g) := by
576	      intro f
577	      rw [← ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact,
578	          ← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff]
579	      exact h_a f
580	    -- ===========================================================================
581	    -- Iter-062 substantive scaffolding (b): outline the local-to-global route.
582	    -- ===========================================================================
583	    -- The closure of the remaining goal proceeds via Mathlib's local-to-global
584	    -- principle for exactness `exact_of_isLocalized_span` applied to the
585	    -- ring `R := Γ(C.left, U)`, the spanning subset `s ⊆ R`, and the
586	    -- `R`-linear maps `(K.sc n).f.hom` and `(K.sc n).g.hom` (treated as
587	    -- `R`-linear via the `Module R ((K.sc n).X i)`-structure obtained from
588	    -- the cochain factor's natural `Γ(C.left, ⋂_x …)`-module structure
589	    -- precomposed with the algebra map `R → Γ(C.left, ⋂_x …)`).
590	    -- The required input is, for each `f ∈ s`, a `Function.Exact`
591	    -- statement on the localised cochain factors at `f`. By the
592	    -- cochain-factor identification chain (iter-057 + iter-058 + iter-059
593	    -- + Mathlib `Localization.localizationLocalization` + product-localisation
594	    -- commutation), the localised `(K.sc n).X i [1/f]` identifies with the
595	    -- slice-cover Čech `.X i` at the same degree, and consequently the
596	    -- localised differential identifies with the slice-cover differential.
597	    -- This identification + `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` transports
598	    -- `h_a_fun f` (which is the slice-cover Čech `Function.Exact` at
599	    -- degree `n`) to `Function.Exact` of the localised differential.
600	    -- The infinite-set subtlety (`s` is `Set`, not `Finset`) is sidestepped
601	    -- because `Ideal.span s = ⊤` already implies a *finite* subset spans
602	    -- the unit ideal — `exact_of_isLocalized_span` only consumes the per-`r`
603	    -- localised exactness data and the spanning hypothesis, not finiteness
604	    -- of `s` directly.

606	    -- Substep (b1) — install the `Module Γ(C.left, U)` instances on each
607	    -- cochain factor `(K.sc n).X i`.
608	    -- ===========================================================================
609	    -- The cochain factor `K.X i = ∏_{x : Fin (i+1) → s} Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k))`
610	    -- carries a `Γ(C.left, U)`-module structure pointwise, since each
611	    -- `Γ(C.left, V)` for `V ≤ U` is a `Γ(C.left, U)`-algebra via the
612	    -- restriction map (iter-058's `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le` supplies
613	    -- the inclusion `V ≤ U`, iter-006's `algebraSection` supplies the
614	    -- algebra structure on each factor).
615	    --
616	    -- ===========================================================================
617	    -- Substep (b2) — exhibit `slice_K.X i` as `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1`
618	    -- of `(K.sc n).X i` for each `f ∈ s`.
619	    -- ===========================================================================
620	    -- The cochain-factor identification chain (iter-057 + iter-058 + iter-059
621	    -- + Mathlib `Localization.localizationLocalization` + product-localisation
622	    -- commutation) gives

624	    --                = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k) ⊓ D(f))`
625	    --                = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k))[1/f]`        (iter-058 + iter-059)
626	    --                = `(∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k)))[1/f]`     (product-loc commutation)
627	    --                = `(K.X i)[1/f]`.
628	    -- The pointwise localisation map (component-wise restriction map
629	    -- `Γ(C.left, V) → Γ(C.left, V')` for `V' = V ⊓ D(f) ≤ V`) is the canonical
630	    -- localisation map and an `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` instance.
631	    --
632	    -- ===========================================================================
633	    -- Substep (c) — apply `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ... h_a_fun`.
634	    -- ===========================================================================
635	    -- With the data from (b1) and (b2), `exact_of_isLocalized_span` packages

640	    -- ===========================================================================

643	    -- ===========================================================================
644	    -- The localisation-commutes-with-products subtlety identified by the
645	    -- iter-061 prover (Attempt 3) is genuine and severe: for the cochain
646	    -- factor `K.X i = ∏ᶜ_{x : Fin (i+1) → s} P.obj (op (∏ᶜ_k 𝒰(x k)))` in

669	      (Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite (s := s)).mp hs

674	    -- transport `s → s₀` (using `hs_fin`); (ii) substep (b2) at the finite
675	    -- cover `s₀` where the product-localisation commutation succeeds; and

683	    --    Function.Exact (K(s₀).sc n).f (K(s₀).sc n).g`
684	    -- under `hs_fin`. A natural framing: there is a *cochain-complex map*

293	namespace AlternatingCofaceMapComplex
294	
295	variable (X Y : CosimplicialObject C)
296	
297	/-- The differential on the alternating coface map complex is the alternate
298	sum of the coface maps -/
299	@[simp]
300	def objD (n : ℕ) : X.obj ⦋n⦌ ⟶ X.obj ⦋n + 1⦌ :=
301	  ∑ i : Fin (n + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • X.δ i
302	
303	theorem d_eq_unop_d (n : ℕ) :
304	    objD X n =
305	      (AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD ((cosimplicialSimplicialEquiv C).functor.obj (op X))
306	          n).unop := by
307	  simp only [objD, AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD, unop_sum, unop_zsmul]
308	  rfl
309	
310	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
311	theorem d_squared (n : ℕ) : objD X n ≫ objD X (n + 1) = 0 := by
312	  simp only [d_eq_unop_d, ← unop_comp, AlternatingFaceMapComplex.d_squared, unop_zero]
313	
314	/-- The alternating coface map complex, on objects -/
315	def obj : CochainComplex C ℕ :=
316	  CochainComplex.of (fun n => X.obj ⦋n⦌) (objD X) (d_squared X)
317	
318	variable {X} {Y}
319	
320	/-- The alternating face map complex, on morphisms -/
321	@[simp]
322	def map (f : X ⟶ Y) : obj X ⟶ obj Y :=
323	  CochainComplex.ofHom _ _ _ _ _ _ (fun n => f.app ⦋n⦌) fun n => by
324	    dsimp
325	    rw [comp_sum, sum_comp]
326	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun x _ => ?_
327	    rw [comp_zsmul, zsmul_comp]
328	    congr 1
329	    symm
330	    apply f.naturality
331	
332	end AlternatingCofaceMapComplex
333	
334	variable (C)
335	
336	/-- The alternating coface map complex, as a functor -/
337	@[simps]
338	def alternatingCofaceMapComplex : CosimplicialObject C ⥤ CochainComplex C ℕ where
339	  obj := AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.obj
340	  map f := AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.map f

module

public import Mathlib.AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproducts.Cech

/-!
# Cech cohomology

Given a family of objects `U : ι → C` in a category `C` that has finite products,
we define a Cech complex functor
`cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ` which sends a presheaf
`P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` in a preadditive category (where products exist) to the cochain
complex which in degree `n` consists of the product, indexed by `i : Fin (n + 1) → ι`,
of the value of `P` on the product of the objects `U (i a)` for `a : Fin (n + 1)`.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe w t v v' u u'

namespace CategoryTheory

open Limits

variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {A : Type u'} [Category.{v'} A] [HasProducts.{w} A]

namespace Limits.FormalCoproduct

open Opposite

variable (E : SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct.{w} C))

/-- Given a simplicial object `E` in the category `FormalCoproduct C`, this is the
functor `(Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CosimplicialObject A` which sends `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` to the
cosimplicial object which sends `⦋n⦌` to the "evaluation" of `P` on `E _⦋n⦌`. -/
@[simps!]
noncomputable def cosimplicialObjectFunctor :
    (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CosimplicialObject A :=
  evalOp.{w} C A ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj E.rightOp

variable [Preadditive A]

/-- Given a simplicial object `E` in the category `FormalCoproduct C`, this is the
functor `(Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ` which sends `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` to the
cochain complex which in degree `n` consists of the "evaluation" of `P` on `E _⦋n⦌`. -/
@[simps!]
noncomputable def cochainComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ :=
  cosimplicialObjectFunctor E ⋙ AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex A

end Limits.FormalCoproduct

variable [HasFiniteProducts C] [Preadditive A] {ι : Type w} (U : ι → C)

/-- Given a family of objects `U : ι → C`, this is the Cech complex functor
`(Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ` which sends a presheaf `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` to the
cochain complex which in degree `n` consists of the product,
indexed by `x : Fin (n + 1) → ι`, of the value of `P` on the product of the
objects `U (x i)` for `i : Fin (n + 1)`. -/
noncomputable def cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ :=
  FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech

end CategoryTheory"}

379	
380	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
381	/-- A presheaf valued in a category `A` with arbitrary products can be extended to the category of
382	formal coproducts. -/
383	@[simps!] def evalOp : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ ((FormalCoproduct.{w} C)ᵒᵖ ⥤ A) where
384	  obj F :=
385	    { obj X := ∏ᶜ fun (i : X.unop.I) ↦ F.obj (op (X.unop.obj i))
386	      map f := Pi.lift fun i ↦ Pi.π _ (f.unop.f i) ≫ F.map (f.unop.φ i).op }
387	  map α := { app f := Pi.map fun i ↦ α.app (op (f.unop.obj i)) }
388	
389	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
390	/-- `evalOp(F)` restricted to the original category (via `incl`) is the original presheaf `F`. -/
391	@[simps!] def evalOpCompInlIsoId :
392	    evalOp C A ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ A).obj (incl C).op ≅ Functor.id (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) :=
393	  NatIso.ofComponents fun F ↦ NatIso.ofComponents fun x ↦
394	    ⟨Pi.π _ PUnit.unit, Pi.lift fun _ ↦ 𝟙 _, by aesop, by simp⟩
395	
396	variable {C A}
397	
398	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
399	/-- `evalOp(F)` preserves arbitrary products. -/
400	def isLimitEvalMapConeCofanOp : IsLimit (((evalOp.{w} C A).obj F).mapCone (cofan.{w} J f).op) where
401	  lift s := Pi.lift fun i ↦ s.π.app ⟨i.1⟩ ≫ Pi.π _ i.2
402	  fac s i := Pi.hom_ext _ _ fun i ↦ by simp [cofan]
403	  uniq s m h := Pi.hom_ext _ _ fun ⟨i₁, i₂⟩ ↦ by simp [← h, cofan]
404	
405	instance : PreservesLimit (Discrete.functor (op ∘ f)) ((evalOp.{w} C A).obj F) :=
406	  ⟨fun hc ↦ ⟨IsLimit.ofIsoLimit (isLimitEvalMapConeCofanOp J f F) ((Cone.functoriality _ _).mapIso
407	    ((Cofan.IsColimit.op (isColimitCofan J f)).uniqueUpToIso hc))⟩⟩
408	
409	end HasProducts

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicTopology.SimplicialObject.Basic
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproducts.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	# The Cech object for formal coproducts
13	
14	Let `C` be a category that has finite products. In this file, we define a
15	functor `cechFunctor : FormalCoproduct C ⥤ SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct C)`
16	which sends a formal coproduct of objects `U j` (for `j : ι`) to the simplicial object
17	which sends `⦋n⦌` to the formal coproduct, indexed by `i : Fin (n + 1) → ι`,
18	of the products of the objects `U (i a)` for all `a : Fin (n + 1)`.
19	
20	-/
21	
22	@[expose] public section
23	
24	universe w t v u
25	
26	namespace CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct
27	
28	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C]
29	
30	/-- Given `U : FormalCoproduct C` and a type `α`, this is the formal coproduct
31	indexed by all `i : α → U.I` of the products of the objects `U.obj (i a)`
32	for all `a : α`. -/
33	@[simps]
34	noncomputable def power (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) (α : Type t)
35	    [HasProductsOfShape α C] : FormalCoproduct.{max w t} C where
36	  I := α → U.I
37	  obj i := ∏ᶜ (U.obj ∘ i)
38	
39	section
40	
41	variable (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) (α : Type) [HasProductsOfShape α C]
42	
43	variable {α} in
44	/-- The projection `U.power α ⟶ U` for each `a : α`. -/
45	@[simps]
46	noncomputable def powerπ (a : α) : U.power α ⟶ U where
47	  f i := i a
48	  φ _ := Pi.π _ a
49	
50	/-- The (limit) fan expressing that `U.power α` is a product of copies of
51	`U` indexed by `α`. -/
52	noncomputable abbrev powerFan :
53	    Fan (fun (_ : α) ↦ U) :=
54	  Fan.mk (U.power α) U.powerπ
55	
56	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
57	/-- `U.power α` identifies to the product of copies of `U` indexed by `α`. -/
58	noncomputable def isLimitPowerFan : IsLimit (U.powerFan α) :=
59	  mkFanLimit _
60	    (fun s ↦
61	      { f i a := (s.proj a).f i
62	        φ i := Pi.lift (fun a ↦ (s.proj a).φ i) })
63	    (fun _ _ ↦ by ext <;> simp)
64	    (fun s m hm ↦ by
65	      obtain ⟨f, φ⟩ := m
66	      obtain rfl : f = fun i a ↦ (s.proj a).f i := by
67	        ext i
68	        dsimp
69	        ext a
70	        exact congr_fun (congr_arg FormalCoproduct.Hom.f (hm a)) i
71	      ext i
72	      · rfl
73	      · dsimp
74	        ext a
75	        specialize hm a
76	        rw [hom_ext_iff] at hm
77	        obtain ⟨_, hm⟩ := hm
78	        simpa using hm i)
79	
80	end

content: 67:noncomputable abbrev sc := (shortComplexFunctor C c i).obj K

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2023 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Joël Riou
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Additive
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ShortComplex.Exact
10	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ShortComplex.Preadditive
11	public import Mathlib.Tactic.NormNum
12	
13	/-!
14	# The short complexes attached to homological complexes
15	
16	In this file, we define a functor
17	`shortComplexFunctor C c i : HomologicalComplex C c ⥤ ShortComplex C`.
18	By definition, the image of a homological complex `K` by this functor
19	is the short complex `K.X (c.prev i) ⟶ K.X i ⟶ K.X (c.next i)`.
20	
21	The homology `K.homology i` of a homological complex `K` in degree `i` is defined as
22	the homology of the short complex `(shortComplexFunctor C c i).obj K`, which can be
23	abbreviated as `K.sc i`.
24	
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Category Limits
30	
31	namespace HomologicalComplex
32	
33	variable (C : Type*) [Category* C] [HasZeroMorphisms C] {ι : Type*} (c : ComplexShape ι)
34	
35	/-- The functor `HomologicalComplex C c ⥤ ShortComplex C` which sends a homological
36	complex `K` to the short complex `K.X i ⟶ K.X j ⟶ K.X k` for arbitrary indices `i`, `j` and `k`. -/
37	@[simps]
38	def shortComplexFunctor' (i j k : ι) : HomologicalComplex C c ⥤ ShortComplex C where
39	  obj K := ShortComplex.mk (K.d i j) (K.d j k) (K.d_comp_d i j k)
40	  map f :=
41	    { τ₁ := f.f i
42	      τ₂ := f.f j
43	      τ₃ := f.f k }
44	
45	/-- The functor `HomologicalComplex C c ⥤ ShortComplex C` which sends a homological
46	complex `K` to the short complex `K.X (c.prev i) ⟶ K.X i ⟶ K.X (c.next i)`. -/
47	@[simps!]
48	noncomputable def shortComplexFunctor (i : ι) :=
49	  shortComplexFunctor' C c (c.prev i) i (c.next i)
50	
51	/-- The natural isomorphism `shortComplexFunctor C c j ≅ shortComplexFunctor' C c i j k`
52	when `c.prev j = i` and `c.next j = k`. -/
53	@[simps!]
54	noncomputable def natIsoSc' (i j k : ι) (hi : c.prev j = i) (hk : c.next j = k) :
55	    shortComplexFunctor C c j ≅ shortComplexFunctor' C c i j k :=
56	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun K => ShortComplex.isoMk (K.XIsoOfEq hi) (Iso.refl _) (K.XIsoOfEq hk)
57	    (by simp) (by simp)) (by cat_disch)
58	
59	variable {C c}
60	
61	variable (K L M : HomologicalComplex C c) (φ : K ⟶ L) (iso : K ≅ L) (ψ : L ⟶ M) (i j k : ι)
62	
63	/-- The short complex `K.X i ⟶ K.X j ⟶ K.X k` for arbitrary indices `i`, `j` and `k`. -/
64	abbrev sc' := (shortComplexFunctor' C c i j k).obj K
65	
66	/-- The short complex `K.X (c.prev i) ⟶ K.X i ⟶ K.X (c.next i)`. -/
67	noncomputable abbrev sc := (shortComplexFunctor C c i).obj K
68	
69	/-- The canonical isomorphism `K.sc j ≅ K.sc' i j k` when `c.prev j = i` and `c.next j = k`. -/
70	noncomputable abbrev isoSc' (hi : c.prev j = i) (hk : c.next j = k) :
71	    K.sc j ≅ K.sc' i j k := (natIsoSc' C c i j k hi hk).app K
72	
73	/-- A homological complex `K` has homology in degree `i` if the associated
74	short complex `K.sc i` has. -/
75	abbrev HasHomology := (K.sc i).HasHomology
76	
77	variable {K L} in
78	include iso in
79	lemma hasHomology_of_iso [K.HasHomology i] : L.HasHomology i :=
80	  ShortComplex.hasHomology_of_iso
81	    ((shortComplexFunctor _ _ i).mapIso iso : K.sc i ≅ L.sc i)
82	
83	section
84	
85	variable [K.HasHomology i]

        -- Step S3 (helper): the only `n`-dependent positivity needed below.  For

        have hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1 = n := by
          rcases n with _ | k
          · simp at hn
          · simp [ComplexShape.prev, ComplexShape.up_Rel]
        -- Step S2 + S3 + S4 (combined unfold).  `dsimp` peels the 5-layer functor
        -- stack `scK₀ → K₀ → cechCochain → cechComplexFunctor → toModuleKSheaf`

        -- `.d` projection to the explicit alternating sum
        --   `∑ i : Fin (prev n + 2), (-1)^i • Pi.lift (Pi.π Z₁ (· ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom) ≫
        --      (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (Pi.lift (Pi.π (basicOpenCover s₀ ∘ _) (δ_i ·)).op)`
        -- composed with an `eqToHom` casting through `hRel`.

        simp [FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor,
          FormalCoproduct.cosimplicialObjectFunctor,

          AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.obj,

        --        chain expected.

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean — iter-081 report

## File status

- **Sorry count: 6** (unchanged from iter-080 — hard cap preserved; target ≤ 5 NOT hit).
- **File compiles cleanly** (no errors); only pre-existing lint warnings remain.
- The iter-080 named-per-i `letI` refactor at L920–949 is **preserved byte-for-byte**.
- The `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L418 is **preserved**.

## Lane 1 target: `h_diff_pi_smul_f` body at L1176 — structural advance, not closed

### Attempt 1 — execute S2+S3+S4 inline (PARTIAL ADVANCE)

- **Approach:** Substantively advance the iter-080 documented recipe.  Establish `hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1 = n` (closes S3 case-split via `rcases n; simp [ComplexShape.prev, ComplexShape.up_Rel]`).  Then execute S2+S3+S4 combined: a 5-layer `dsimp` peels `scK₀ → K₀ → cechCochain → cechComplexFunctor → toModuleKSheaf`; a full `simp` with `[FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor, ..., AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD, CochainComplex.of, FormalCoproduct.evalOp, e₁, e₂, ModuleCat.piIsoPi, dif_pos hRel]` collapses the `K₀.d` projection through the `CochainComplex.of`-built differential, **exposing the explicit alternating-sum form**.
- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — file compiles; sorry remains at the per-summand R-linearity step (S5–S8).  The goal at the sorry is now (both sides):
  ```
  (piIsoPi Z₂).hom (eqToHom _ ∘ₗ
      (∑ i : Fin (prev n + 2), (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (fun j' ↦
         Pi.π Z₁ (j' ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom) ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (Pi.lift _).op)))
    ((piIsoPi Z₁).symm <r • y or y>) j
  ```
  (vs. RHS having an outer `r • ·` at the j-component level).
- **Key insight:** The iter-080 sorry-rationale block guessed that this would require
  `CochainComplex.of_d_eq_succ` (non-existent in Mathlib).  In practice, the `dif_pos hRel` rewrite **inside a full `simp` invocation** flushes `CochainComplex.of_d` automatically — once `CochainComplex.of` is in the simp set, simp unfolds the `d`-projection to its `if h : i + 1 = j then ... else 0` form, and `dif_pos hRel` selects the `then` branch.  This is what bridges S2 to S3 to S4 in one tactic step.
- **Lemmas used / discovered:**
  - `ComplexShape.prev`, `ComplexShape.up_Rel` — for `hRel`.
  - `FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor`, `cosimplicialObjectFunctor` — fully unfold to `evalOp ⋙ alternatingCofaceMapComplex` (Mathlib defs).
  - `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex`, `AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.obj`, `.objD`, `.map`, `CochainComplex.of` — for S4.
  - `FormalCoproduct.evalOp` — exposes `(evalOp.obj P).obj X = ∏ᶜ (fun i => P.obj (op (X.unop.obj i)))`.
  - `ModuleCat.piIsoPi`, `dif_pos hRel` — collapse the `K₀.d` projection.

### Attempt 2 — close S5–S8 (NOT ATTEMPTED — time budget)

The remaining S5–S8 chain (per the inline rationale block in the body) is:

1. **S5:** Distribute `e₂ = (piIsoPi Z₂).hom` and the alternating sum through `Pi.lift` to surface per-`(i, j')` summands at the j-component.  Need `Pi.π Z₁ k (e₁.symm z) = z k` (from `piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι`) to reduce the inner argument, and `eqToHom_app` to commute the eqToHom cast through the sum.
2. **S6:** `Finset.smul_sum` distributes `r •` across the RHS sum.
3. **S7:** Per-summand: each summand at fixed `i, j'` is `(toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i).op . hom (z (j' ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))` where `z = e₁.symm (r • y)`.  For `z = r • y`, the inner is `(presheaf.map (V_{j'∘δ_i} ≤ U).op).hom r * y (j' ∘ δ_i)` by `perI₁`.  Apply `RingHom.map_mul` (the `.hom` of a `presheaf.map (V_{j'} ≤ V_{j'∘δ_i}).op` is a ring-hom), then `← presheaf.map_comp` collapses two restrictions into one.
4. **S8:** Per-summand identity closes by `rfl`/`ring`.

The detailed recipe is now inline in the body (replacing the iter-080 rationale block).  Estimated ~30 LOC for full closure given that S2+S3+S4 are now done.

## Dead-end warnings

- **DO NOT** attempt `simp only` (instead of full `simp`) for the S2+S3+S4 unfold — `simp only` fails to collapse `CochainComplex.of_d` because it requires extra elaboration that only the full simp engine performs.
- **DO NOT** try to use `CochainComplex.of_d_eq_succ` — it does not exist in Mathlib.  Use `dif_pos hRel` inside a full `simp` invocation instead.
- **DO NOT** try `rw [dif_pos hRel]` after a `simp only` chain — the conditional `dite` won't be present in the goal yet, only the (still wrapped) `K₀.d` projection.

## Status: PARTIAL ADVANCE — substantive structural progress on top of iter-080

- **Net sorry count this iter: 6 → 6** (no closure; hard cap preserved).
- **Body advance (substantive):** S2+S3+S4 from the PROGRESS.md recipe are now **executed inline**; the goal at the sorry exposes the explicit alternating-sum form.  This is the deepest the proof has reached across iter-072 to iter-081.
- **Next iteration target:** Close S5–S8 from the now-exposed alternating-sum form.  The remaining work is local per-summand R-linearity using `RingHom.map_mul` + `← presheaf.map_comp`, after distributing through `e₂` and `Pi.lift` (using `piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι` for the inverse direction).

## Blueprint marker

- The per-summand R-linearity is internal to `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf` (no top-level `\lean{...}` label).  No marker action needed.
- The top-level `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf` retains its 6 sorries; the marker remains as before.

## Other sorries (out of scope this iter, unchanged)

- L502: substep (a) extra-degeneracy (dead-end).
- L826: substep (i) Čech-cohomology refinement transport (dead-end).
- L854: substep (a) for `s₀` (dead-end).
- L1241: `g_R.map_smul'` (gated on `h_diff_pi_smul_g` re-introduction, downstream of this lane).
- L1270: `h_loc_exact` (needs `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` infrastructure; iter-082+).

9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.

The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.

48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps

15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.

18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures.
